title: EB-5 petition package manifest
description: Written as _eb5_manifest.json at the root of a package scaffolded by the eb5-prepare skill. Consumed by eb5-gapcheck to know what SHOULD exist and — critically — which files are non-sensitive 'scaffold' files that are safe to read in full. Files NOT listed under scaffold_files are treated as opaque (metadata only): eb5-gapcheck must never read, parse, quote, or transmit their contents.

project_label.description: Non-sensitive label only (project / RC name or a chosen package name). Never an investor's personal data.

scaffold_marker.description: The exact first-line marker every generated scaffold file carries. eb5-gapcheck uses it as a secondary safe-to-read signal when no manifest is present.

sof_paths.description: Selected source-of-funds CATEGORIES (not amounts or account data).

foreign_language_docs.description: If true, certified-translation items are expected.

dependents.description: Whether dependent (spouse/children) document slots are expected.

folders.description: Folders that should exist (relative to package root).

scaffold_files.description: AUTHORITATIVE allow-list of non-sensitive files that eb5-gapcheck may read in full. Anything not in this list is metadata-only.

working_docs.description: Files the user is expected to FILL from a template (e.g. SOF_Narrative.md). May contain figures/PII once filled — eb5-gapcheck may only scan them for remaining placeholder tokens, never read their content.

policy.description: Human-readable restatement of the hard rule: structure-only inspection; never read/echo/transmit sensitive document contents.

sensitive_globs.description: Path/extension globs whose CONTENTS must never be read. Folders 01/02/03 and common document/spreadsheet/image formats.

builder_token.description: Token form the builder must fully substitute (e.g. '{{...}}'). Any remaining occurrence is a build defect.

placeholder_tokens.description: User-fill placeholder forms (e.g. '[[FILL'). Remaining occurrences mean a template is incomplete.
